feat: implement com.microsoft::SkipLayerNormalization + merge upstream operator additions (#689)

* feat: implement com.microsoft::SkipLayerNormalization operator

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/emmtrix/emx-onnx-cgen/sessions/90152413-23c3-4435-99da-fe53aaf74b57

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

* lowering: fix LayerNormalization scale broadcast rank and RotaryEmbedding input order

LayerNormalization (_ensure_broadcastable):
- Relax scale/bias rank check: allow any rank <= input_rank, with broadcast
  compatibility checked right-aligned against the full input shape (not just
  the normalized sub-shape). This matches the ONNX spec which states scale/bias
  must be unidirectionally broadcastable to X.

LayerNormalizationOp.emit (nn.py):
- Compute scale_index_vars / bias_index_vars right-aligned with all loop vars
  (prefix + norm), so that scale tensors with leading batch/prefix dims work
  correctly in the generated C.
- Handle scalar scale/bias (rank 0) by returning ["0"] as the single index
  into the C array declared as [1].

RotaryEmbedding (rotary_embedding.py):
- Fix input order: the operator signature is input, [position_ids], cos_cache,
  sin_cache. The previous code assumed input, cos, sin, [position_ids].
- For 3D inputs without a num_heads attribute, infer num_heads from the
  cos/sin cache shape: head_size = rotary_dim = 2 * cos_shape[-1],
  num_heads = hidden / head_size.
- Allow position_ids of shape (1,) in addition to (batch, seq_len). Shape (1,)
  is a starting-position offset; the actual position for sequence index s is
  position_ids[0] + s.

rotary_embedding_op.c.j2:
- Emit pos = position_ids[0] + s for the broadcast (offset) case.

Update expected_error snapshots for 15 LayerNormalization and 12 RotaryEmbedding
test models whose error type changed from a lowering/codegen failure to a
numerical result (or pass).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

SKIPPED. Switch to JUnit 5 Assumptions.assumeTrue(false, message) which
causes the test runner to mark scenarios as aborted/skipped when the
Catala CLI is not installed on PATH.

Fixes GitHub Actions CI failure on:
- ga_1_4_3_opskrivning.catala_da compiles without errors
- ga_1_4_4_nedskrivning.catala_da compiles without errors

NFR mandate (SPEC-P054 §6): 'if CLI not found, skip with a clear
message; do NOT fail the build if CLI is absent.'

commit.message:
docs(adr): ADR 0032 — Catala as formal compliance verification layer

Formalizes the decision to use Catala (Inria/ICFP) as an oracle between
the juridisk vejledning and the Gherkin test suite for Tier A G.A. sections.

Key points:
- Catala encodes legal rules with article anchors in Danish (.catala_da)
- Acts as oracle: Catala discrepancies override Gherkin, not vice versa
- Tier A/B/C classification for scope management
- Research spike pattern (P054 template) with Go/No-Go gate
- Does NOT replace Gherkin, Drools, or the Java runtime
- Repository conventions for catala/ directory structure

References: Merigoux et al. (2021) DOI 10.1007/s10506-022-09328-5,
P054 (Go outcome), P069-P072 spike companions, ADR 0015, ADR 0031,
G.A. v3.16 (2026-03-28)

commit.message:
Fix message sent to wrong chat when switching during worktree creation

The bug: when a user sends a message in a new worktree chat and switches
to another chat before CreateWorktreeAsync completes, the message and
its streaming response end up in the wrong chat.

Root cause: SendMessage() used CurrentChat (a mutable property) and
_activeSession (a shared field) throughout the method. Both get
overwritten when LoadChatAsync runs for the chat the user switches to.
The streaming event handlers gate UI updates on _activeSession == session,
so when EnsureSessionAsync overwrites _activeSession for the background
chat, the response pollutes the displayed chat's UI.

Fix:
1. Capture CurrentChat into a local 'targetChat' before the async
   worktree creation gap and use it for all Chat model operations.
2. After EnsureSessionAsync sets up the session for targetChat, capture
   it as 'sendSession' and call RestoreActiveSessionIfSwitched to point
   _activeSession back to the displayed chat's session. This ensures
   streaming events for the background send don't touch the UI.
3. Use sendSession for CaptureExpectedSessionUserMessageCountAsync and
   SendAsync calls, including retry paths.
4. Update HandleSendError to accept an explicit Chat parameter so error
   messages target the correct chat. Guard view-level property updates
   behind CurrentChat identity checks.
5. Fix TryRecoverTransportSendAsync to look up the session from
   _sessionCache instead of using _activeSession, which may have been
   restored to the displayed chat.
6. Extract RestoreActiveSessionIfSwitched helper and call it after every
   code path that sets _activeSession for a background chat (session
   setup, retry, transport recovery).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix streaming text getting stuck during chat responses

Three root causes fixed:

1. UiThrottler dispatched flushes at DispatcherPriority.Background,
   which got starved by higher-priority layout/render work. Changed
   default to DispatcherPriority.Normal.

2. UiThrottler and StrataMarkdown had no error recovery in their
   async scheduling paths — an unexpected exception would leave
   _scheduled/_rebuildQueued stuck as true forever, permanently
   blocking all future updates.

3. The session event handler (~1000 lines) had no try-catch wrapper.
   If any case handler threw, the SDK would stop delivering events,
   leaving IsStreaming/IsBusy stuck permanently with no error shown.

Added 10 regression tests covering priority ordering, starvation
resistance, cancel+request patterns, and StrataMarkdown streaming.
